description_long: This is a shallow, wide-mouthed ceramic bowl with a circular base and two horizontally aligned handles on opposite sides. The exterior surface is adorned with a painted frieze featuring human figures and possibly mythological or ceremonial scenes, rendered in a black-figure style reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery. The bowl's body is primarily a reddish-brown terracotta color, with the painted figures and decorative bands in black and some faded highlights. The handles are slightly curved and show some darkening, possibly from use or age. The interior is plain, with a smooth finish and a few minor surface marks. The overall form and decoration suggest it is both functional and decorative, likely used for serving or ritual purposes.
description: A wide, shallow terracotta bowl with two handles and black-figure painted scenes around the exterior. The bowl features detailed human motifs and a smooth, reddish-brown finish.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from the side, highlighting the left handle and a section of the painted frieze with seated and standing figures.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the right handle and reveals more of the painted figures, including a standing figure and additional decorative details.
description_view_2: The bowl is shown with both handles visible, focusing on the opposite side of the painted frieze and the curvature of the rim.
description_view_3: The left handle is more prominent, and the painted figures are clearly visible, with the bowl's interior and rim also in view.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Black-figure painted scenes of human figures and possible mythological motifs encircle the exterior surface.
